name: Transit-Oriented Development[beta]
author: hsullivan1201
description:
This mod lets you add new pops to the map by building development around your stations. It looks at your transit network, ridership, and demand, figures out where good service could actually support growth, and lets you drop new residents and jobs into those areas.

The dashboard

- Opens from the toolbar (the TOD button)
- Floats and you can drag it wherever, stays out of the way while you play
- Ranks your station areas a few ways:
- where there's room for more housing
- where there's room for more jobs
- spots that are already dense and well-served
- dense spots your trains aren't really reaching

Building stuff
 
- Pick a station, then kick off a development deal there
- Housing, jobs, or a mix, in small/medium/large
- Builds out over 1 to 3 game days, adding new pops (residents and jobs) into the area around the station (about a 500m walk)
- You can also just click a station on the map while the dashboard's open to select it
- Whatever's selected gets a colored ring on the map so you can see the walkshed

Good to know

- Scores are tuned per city from what's actually going on in your map, so think "best spots in this city right now" rather than fixed letter grades
- It's early and kinda beta, so stuff might break
- Saving is a little janky right now: deals are supposed to stick to each save slot but don't always come back clean after a load, so don't be shocked if things look a bit off
- There's a Diagnostics section in the dashboard if you want to peek at what the save stuff is doing
